summary.intent: Fix multiple bugs in the Gossamer VS Code extension (server lifecycle, schema push, checkpoint indexing, multi-repo DB routing) and add new features (agent brand colors, copy-on-hover, markdown links, live session detection, checkpoint remote support, author attribution from shadow branches).
summary.outcome: All targeted bugs were fixed and features shipped with clean builds throughout. Key wins: schema push now executes all 56 statements instead of zero, each repo has its own Prisma client, sessions sort by workspace relevance, author attribution falls back through checkpoint → shadow branch → local git config, and the UI correctly renders markdown links, agent colors, and copy buttons.

repo: pushSchema splits schema.sql on ';\n' but every chunk starts with a '-- CreateTable' comment, so the old '!s.startsWith("--")' filter silently discarded all 56 CREATE TABLE statements.
The server previously held a single global `db` Prisma client bound to the startup --repo-dir; every endpoint that needed a different repo's data returned empty results.

Entire.io writes session transcripts live to .entire/metadata/<sessionId>/full.jsonl on disk, but only commits them to entire/checkpoints/v1 at checkpoint boundaries—causing multi-minute delay before Gossamer sees new sessions.

gitlog npm package defaults to nameStatus:true (--name-status), causing execFile to throw ERR_CHILD_PROCESS_STDIO_MAXBUFFER on large repos; the error was swallowed silently inside saveGitOidMapping, producing zero git OID mappings.
The entire/checkpoints/v1 worktree was created inside the user's repo at .gossamer/checkpoints/, interfering with their working tree; it should live at ~/.gossamer/checkpoints/<repo-name>/.
ShadowSession rows had no gitUserName/gitUserEmail columns; the server fell back to the local machine's git config, mis-attributing other people's sessions.
Entire hooks are configured in the agent's .claude/settings.json, not in Gossamer or .entire/settings.json; without them Entire never writes to full.jsonl.
The gitlog branch search in findCommitForCheckpoint is limited to 10,000 commits but the real blocker was maxBuffer overflow, not the count limit.
The .git/shallow file was truncating entire/checkpoints/v1 history to 1 commit; unshallowing revealed 203 commits.
macOS com.apple.provenance extended attribute on SQLite files caused 'disk I/O error'; fix is to evict the cached Prisma client, delete the file, and retry.
The dbCache in getDb means a broken Prisma client persists in-process even after deleting the file; evictDb() must be called before retrying.
When multiple VS Code windows activate simultaneously, each tries to spawn its own server; the EADDRINUSE losers never recover because the static GossamerPanel.server variable is per-process.

code.finding: pushSchema was only called for the primary DB (CLI arg); secondary repos from config were never schema-migrated at startup.
Session sort used server's startup repoDir for workspace-first ordering; switching to git remote URL comparison makes cross-machine sorting correct.
cpUser?.gitUserName ?? gitUser.name: the ?? operator passes empty string through, so the server's own git user name overwrote other people's blank attribution.
findCommitForCheckpoint searched for 'Entire-Checkpoint:' trailer but the actual commit format on the checkpoint branch uses 'Checkpoint:' in the subject—these are different things.
nameStatus: false and execOptions: { maxBuffer: 100MB } must be passed to gitlog; defaults caused silent buffer-overflow failures on repos with 10k+ commits.
seenBlobShas check skips re-indexing when blob SHA is unchanged; existing ShadowSession rows with no gitUserName were never backfilled without an explicit skip-bypass.

In-parser highlighting via HighlightTermsCtx broke markdown on edge cases (e.g. **bold** showed asterisks). Switched to post-render TreeWalker DOM walk: render markdown normally then wrap text node matches in <mark> elements.
ClaudeTurnCard toolsExpanded/thinkingExpanded initialized to false unconditionally; search hits inside tool groups were invisible because the outer collapse was closed.

SessionDetailPanel never injected window.__GOSSAMER_REPO_PATH__, so the webview api.ts localPath param was always empty string, causing every API call to probe all DBs instead of targeting the right one.

workflow: Test schema correctness by querying the SQLite DB directly with sqlite3 after running the server, not just by checking if the server starts.
When a fix appears to not work, verify that the compiled dist/ reflects the fix before assuming the logic is wrong.
Use 'git -C <worktreePath> reset --hard <ref>' to sync worktrees; for bare repos refs live at refs/heads/* not refs/remotes/origin/*.
Wrap optional CLI tool hooks with 'command -v tool >/dev/null 2>&1 && tool ... || true' to allow checked-in hooks that silently skip when the tool isn't installed.
Post-render DOM walking (TreeWalker) is more robust than parser-level injection for search highlighting because it decouples highlighting from the rendering pipeline entirely.

friction: pushSchema bug caused repeated 'no such table' failures across multiple debugging rounds because the root cause (comment-filter discarding all statements) was non-obvious and the server logs showed 'schema ready' before failing.
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr silently caused SQLite disk I/O errors with no clear error message pointing to the xattr.
gitlog's maxBuffer overflow was caught by a bare catch block in saveGitOidMapping, producing zero git OID mappings with no log output to diagnose it.
The installed VSIX (bundled-server) runs independently of the dev repo dist/, causing confusion where code fixes appeared not to work.
Multiple VS Code windows each trying to spawn their own server with no inter-process coordination; the EADDRINUSE orphan-kill logic added earlier was later reverted causing the issue to recur.
Session author not showing because (a) no columns existed, (b) empty string passed ?? check, and (c) the session never landed on a surviving shadow branch—three separate root causes stacked.
UI text shifting on hover due to conditional-mount copy button (three separate files had the same bug).
open_items: SessionDetailPanel still does not set window.__GOSSAMER_REPO_PATH__, so fetchSession/fetchLogEvents/fetchCheckpoints never send ?localPath= and always fall back to probing all DBs.
Multi-window server spawn conflict not resolved: installed extension still tries to spawn its own server even when one is already running on the port.
The 'fork via entire resume' flow opens a terminal but cannot auto-open a session detail panel for the newly created session because the new session ID isn't known at click time.
Cross-node search term highlighting (terms spanning across inline markdown nodes like bold/link boundaries) is still not supported by the TreeWalker approach.
Live session indexing from .entire/metadata/ (3-second poll + fs.watch) was implemented and then reverted; new sessions still have a delay until Entire commits to a shadow branch.

Entire CLI not writing new events to full.jsonl in cosmos-graphql (hooks missing from .claude/settings.json); the VS Code warning popup was added but the underlying issue of hooks not being checked in persists for that repo.

ShadowSession backfill only triggers when blob SHA changes; stranded sessions (shadow branch deleted, no checkpoint commit) rely solely on the local git config fallback which attributes to the current machine's user regardless of who actually ran the session.
The checkpoint branch is fetched shallowly in some cases (shallow boundary in .git/shallow); this doesn't affect file-tree indexing but does break findCommitForCheckpoint's git log traversal.
